enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks
- Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
enriched_requirements.object: immediate effect

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Regional Rural Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'RRBs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'RRB').
enriched_requirements.object: Rural Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'RRBs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'RRB')

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The maintenance of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) shall be reported to the RBI under
the statutory return Form A.
enriched_requirements.object: RBI under the statutory return Form A

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The maintenance of Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) shall be reported to the RBI
under the statutory return Form VIII.
enriched_requirements.object: RBI under the statutory return Form VIII

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meaning assigned to them below:
(1) 'Aggregate Deposits' shall mean aggregation of demand and time deposits.
enriched_requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Aggregate Deposits' shall mean aggregation of demand and time deposits

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Apportionment of Saving Bank Account into demand liability and time liability': a
bank shall undertake the apportionment of Saving Bank Account into demand liability
and time liability as per the following procedure:
Explanation:
Explanation: Money at Call and Short Notice from outside the banking system shall
be shown against liability to others.
enriched_requirements.object: Saving Bank Account into demand liability and time liability as per the following procedure: Explanation: Explanation:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(14) 2'Fortnight' means the period from the first day to the fifteenth day of each
calendar month or sixteenth day to the last day of each calendar month, both days
inclusive.
enriched_requirements.object: first day to the fifteenth day of each calendar month or sixteenth day to the last day of each calendar month, both days inclusive

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(15) 'Investment in India' shall consist of investment in approved government
securities and other approved securities (as explained below).
enriched_requirements.object: approved government securities and other approved securities (as explained below)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These shall include
both encumbered and unencumbered securities as per the bank's investment book.
enriched_requirements.object: unencumbered securities as per the bank's investment book

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(16) 'Investment in India in other Government Securities' shall mean Investment in
Government securities which are not approved securities (such as SDLs issued as
UDAY bonds).
enriched_requirements.object: securities which are not approved securities (such as SDLs issued as UDAY bonds)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(17) 'Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF)' shall mean fixed and variable rate Repo
operations (for injection of liquidity) and reverse repo operations (for absorption of
liquidity) conducted by the Reserve Bank of India from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: rate Repo operations (for injection of liquidity) and reverse repo operations (for absorption of liquidity) conducted by the R

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(18) 'Marginal standing facility' shall mean the facility under which the eligible banks
can avail liquidity support from the Reserve Bank against excess SLR holdings.
enriched_requirements.object: which the eligible banks can avail liquidity support from the Reserve Bank against excess SLR holdings

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(21) 'Other Approved Securities' shall mean Government Securities, other than the
securities mentioned in paragraph 6(3) above, subject to the condition that they are
notified as approved securities.
enriched_requirements.object: than the securities mentioned in paragraph 6(3) above

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(22) 'Other Demand and Time Liabilities (ODTL)' shall include the following:
Explanation:

(i) A bank is required to calculate the proportion of its savings bank deposits as at
the close of business on March 31 and September 30, into demand and time
liabilities in terms of Regulation 7 of The Reserve Bank of India Scheduled
Banks' Regulations, 1951.
enriched_requirements.object: of its savings bank deposits as at the close of business on March 31 and September 30, into demand and time liabilities in

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The average of the minimum balances maintained (in each account) in each of
the months during the half year period shall be treated by the bank as the amount
representing the "time liability" portion of the savings bank deposits.
enriched_requirements.object: bank as the amount representing the "time liability" portion of the savings bank deposits

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) The proportions of demand and time liabilities so obtained for each half year
shall be applied for arriving at demand and time liabilities components of savings
bank deposits for all reporting fortnights during the next half year.
enriched_requirements.object: at demand and time liabilities components of savings bank deposits for all reporting fortnights during the next half year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Approved Securities/SLR securities': Following securities shall be considered as
approved securities (approved securities are commonly known as SLR securities):
enriched_requirements.object: securities (approved securities are commonly known as SLR securities):

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Any other instrument as may be notified by the Reserve Bank of India (as and
when prescribed).
enriched_requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (as and when prescribed)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every RRB shall maintain in India by way of cash reserve, a sum equivalent to
such percent of the total of its NDTL in India, in such manner and for such dates, as
the Reserve Bank in terms of Section 42(1) of the RBI Act, 1934 and Section 18(1) of
BR Act, 1949, may specify, by notification in the Official Gazette, from time to time
having regard to the needs of securing the monetary stability in the country.
enriched_requirements.object: way of cash reserve, a sum equivalent to such percent of the total of its NDTL in India, in such manner and for such dates, as th

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: In terms of Section 42(1A) of RBI Act, 1934, the Reserve Bank may require the

scheduled banks to maintain, in addition to the balances prescribed under Section
42(1) of the Act, an additional average daily balance, the amount of which shall not be
less than the rate specified by the Reserve Bank in the notification published in the
Gazette of India from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: rate specified by the Reserve Bank in the notification published in the Gazette of India from time to time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that such additional balance shall be calculated with reference to the excess
of the total of NDTL of the bank as shown in the Returns referred to in Section 42(2)
of the RBI Act, 1934 over the total of its NDTL at the close of the business on the date
specified in the notification.
enriched_requirements.object: to the excess of the total of NDTL of the bank as shown in the Returns referred to in Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every scheduled bank shall maintain in India with the Reserve Bank, an average
daily balance, the amount of which shall not be less than 3.75 per cent, 3.5 per cent,

Every scheduled bank shall maintain minimum CRR of not less than 90 percent of
the required CRR on all days during the reporting fortnight, in such a manner that the
average of CRR maintained daily shall not be less than the CRR prescribed by the
Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: CRR prescribed by the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of these Directions, the Reserve Bank may specify from time to
time with reference to any transaction or class of transactions that such transaction or
transactions shall be regarded as liability in India of a bank.
enriched_requirements.object: in India of a bank

conditions:
If any question arises as to whether any transaction or transactions shall be
regarded
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If any question arises as to whether any transaction or transactions shall be
regarded, for the purpose of these Directions, as liability in India of a bank, the bank
shall approach the RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: purpose of these Directions, as liability in India of a bank, the bank shall approach the RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The decision of the Reserve Bank thereon shall be final.

Loans / borrowings from abroad by banks in India shall be reckoned as 'liabilities
to others' and shall be subject to reserve requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: to others' and shall be subject to reserve requirements

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Upper Tier II instruments raised and maintained in India / abroad shall be reckoned
as liability for the computation of NDTL for the purpose of reserve requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: for the computation of NDTL for the purpose of reserve requirements

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The balance amount in respect of the drafts issued by the accepting bank on its
correspondent bank under the Remittance Facilities Scheme and remaining unpaid
shall be reckoned as 'Liability to others in India' for the computation of NDTL.
enriched_requirements.object: to others in India' for the computation of NDTL

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
amount received by correspondent banks shall be reckoned as 'Liability to the Banking
System' and this liability may be netted off by the correspondent banks against the
inter-bank assets.
enriched_requirements.object: to the Banking System' and this liability may be netted off by the correspondent banks against the inter-bank assets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Sums placed by banks for issuing drafts / interest / dividend warrants shall be
treated as 'Assets with banking system' and banks shall have the option to net them
off from their inter-bank liabilities.
enriched_requirements.object: with banking system' and banks shall have the option to net them off from their inter-bank liabilities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
scheduled banks in respect of their savings bank deposits on the basis of the position
as at the close of business on 30th September and 31st March every year shall
continue with interest application on savings bank deposits on a daily product basis.
enriched_requirements.object: on savings bank deposits on a daily product basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The liabilities mentioned below shall not form part of liabilities of a bank for the
purpose of CRR and SLR:
(1) Paid up capital, reserves, borrowings through instruments qualifying for Tier 1 and
<image>
additional Tier 1 capital;
enriched_requirements.object: of a bank for the purpose of CRR and SLR: (1) Paid up capital, reserves, borrowings through instruments qualifying for Tier 1

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the funds collected by various branches of the bank or other banks for
the issue and held pending finalisation of allotment of the additional Tier 1 preference
shares shall have to be taken into account for the purpose of calculation of reserve
requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: into account for the purpose of calculation of reserve requirements

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(f) any other financial institution notified by the Central Government in this behalf
shall be reduced by the aggregate of the liabilities of all such banks and
institutions to the scheduled bank.
enriched_requirements.object: aggregate of the liabilities of all such banks and institutions to the scheduled bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Loans out of Foreign Currency Non-Resident Accounts (Banks), (FCNR [B]
Deposits Scheme) and Inter-Bank Foreign Currency (IBFC) deposits shall be included
as part of bank credit for the purpose of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: of bank credit for the purpose of these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks shall use the
conversion rate announced by Financial Benchmarks India Private Limited (FBIL) for
the purpose of converting foreign assets / liabilities for reporting in the statutory returns
mentioned at paragraphs 4 and 5 of these directions.
enriched_requirements.object: announced by Financial Benchmarks India Private Limited (FBIL) for the purpose of converting foreign assets / liabilities for

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For conversion of assets /
liabilities in other currencies, banks may use New York Closing Rate pertaining to the
day end of the reporting day for converting such currencies into USD and then use the
reference rate of FBIL for USD/INR for the same day for conversion into INR.
enriched_requirements.object: Rate pertaining to the day end of the reporting day for converting such currencies into USD and then use the reference rate of FB

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every bank, in addition to the cash reserves which it is required to maintain under
these Directions, shall maintain in India, assets, the value of which shall not be less
than such percentage not exceeding 40 percent of the total of its demand and time
liabilities in India as on the last day of the second preceding fortnight as the Reserve
Bank may, by notification in the Official Gazette, specify from time to time and such
assets shall be maintained in such form and such manner, as may be specified in such
notification.
enriched_requirements.object: percentage not exceeding 40 percent of the total of its demand and time liabilities in India as on the last day of the second preced

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every RRB shall maintain in India assets (hereinafter referred to as 'SLR assets')
the value of which shall not, at the close of business on any day, be less than 18 per
cent of its total net demand and time liabilities in India as on the last day of the second
preceding fortnight in accordance with the method of valuation specified by RBI from
time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: of business on any day, be less than 18 per cent of its total net demand and time liabilities in India as on the last day of the second

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks permitted by Reserve Bank shall have the option to participate in the
Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) Scheme introduced by the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: participate in the Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) Scheme introduced by the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
features of the scheme are:
(1) The eligible banks shall have the option to borrow up to two percent of their
respective NDTL outstanding at the end of the second preceding fortnight.
enriched_requirements.object: borrow up to two percent of their respective NDTL outstanding at the end of the second preceding fortnight

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The eligible entities shall also continue to access overnight funds under this facility
against their excess SLR holdings.
enriched_requirements.object: overnight funds under this facility against their excess SLR holdings

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) In the event of a bank's SLR holding falling below the statutory requirement up to
two percent of its NDTL, the bank shall not have the obligation to seek a specific waiver
for default in SLR compliance arising out of use of this facility in terms of notification
issued under sub section (2A) of Section 24 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
enriched_requirements.object: seek a specific waiver for default in SLR compliance arising out of use of this facility in terms of notification issued under

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
SLR assets shall be maintained by RRBs as under:
(1) Cash, or;
enriched_requirements.object: as under: (1) Cash, or

conditions: Provided that the instruments referred to in items (3)(i) to (3)(iii) mentioned above
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the instruments referred to in items (3)(i) to (3)(iii) mentioned above,
that have been acquired from Reserve Bank of India under reverse repo, shall be
considered as eligible assets for SLR maintenance.
enriched_requirements.object: assets for SLR maintenance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that the following SLR-securities shall not be treated as
encumbered for the purpose of maintenance of SLR assets, namely:
enriched_requirements.object: for the purpose of maintenance of SLR assets, namely:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Funds borrowed under repo including tri-party repo in government securities
shall be exempted from CRR / SLR computation and the security acquired under
repo shall be eligible for SLR provided the security is primarily eligible for SLR
as per the provisions of the Act under which it is required to be maintained.
enriched_requirements.object: / SLR computation and the security acquired under repo shall be eligible for SLR provided the security is primarily eligible for

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Borrowings by a bank through repo in corporate bonds and debentures shall be
reckoned as liabilities for Cash Reserve Ratio / Statutory Liquidity Ratio
requirement and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking system, they
shall be netted as per Section 42 (1) (d) of the RBI Act, 1934.
enriched_requirements.object: for Cash Reserve Ratio / Statutory Liquidity Ratio requirement and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking syst

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) All banks shall maintain investments in Government Securities only in
Subsidiary General Ledger (SGL) Accounts with Reserve Bank or in CSGL
Accounts of scheduled commercial banks, Primary Dealers (PDs), State Co-
operative Banks, and Stock Holding Corporation of India Ltd.
enriched_requirements.object: Securities only in Subsidiary General Ledger (SGL) Accounts with Reserve Bank or in CSGL Accounts of scheduled comm

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(v) 6Banks shall report the SDF balances held by banks with RBI in Form VIII, as it
is an eligible asset for SLR maintenance.
enriched_requirements.object: held by banks with RBI in Form VIII, as it is an eligible asset for SLR maintenance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The balances held by banks with RBI
under the SDF shall not be eligible for Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) maintenance.
enriched_requirements.object: Reserve Ratio (CRR) maintenance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The cash management bill shall be treated as Government of India Treasury
Bill and thus be reckoned as SLR security.
enriched_requirements.object: of India Treasury Bill and thus be reckoned as SLR security

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The procedure for computation of NDTL for SLR shall be as below:
(1) Total NDTL for the purpose of SLR under Section 24 (2A) of Banking Regulation
Act, 1949, shall be computed on the similar procedure as followed for CRR.
enriched_requirements.object: Total NDTL for the purpose of SLR under Section 24 (2A) of Banking Regulation Act, 1949, shall be computed on the similar procedure a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (2) The liabilities mentioned under paragraph 19 of these Directions shall not form

(3) SCBs are required to include inter-bank term deposits / term borrowing liabilities
of all maturities in 'Liabilities to the Banking System'.
enriched_requirements.object: deposits / term borrowing liabilities of all maturities in 'Liabilities to the Banking System'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Banks shall include their inter-bank assets of term deposits and term lending of
all maturities in 'Assets with the Banking System' for computation of NDTL for SLR
purpose.
enriched_requirements.object: of term deposits and term lending of all maturities in 'Assets with the Banking System' for computation of NDTL for SL

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Classification and valuation of approved securities shall be in accordance with the
extant instructions in Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Classification,
Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025, as applicable.
enriched_requirements.object: the extant instructions in Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment P

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Under Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934, every RRB shall submit to Reserve
Bank a Return in Form 'A' (Annex I), at the close of business on 9 last day of each
fortnight within five days after the date of the relevant fortnight to which it relates.
enriched_requirements.object: a Return in Form 'A' (Annex I), at the close of business on 9 last day of each fortnight within five days after the date of the

conditions:
Where such last day of each fortnight is a public holiday under the Negotiable
Instruments Act
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where such last day of each fortnight is a public holiday under the Negotiable
Instruments Act, 1881, for one or more offices of the bank, the Return shall give at the
close of business of the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office or
offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of such fortnight.
enriched_requirements.object: of business of the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate t

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Return in Form 'A', shall be submitted to Reserve Bank along with:
(1) the Memorandum to the Return in Form 'A' giving details of paid-up capital,
reserves, time deposits comprising short-term (of contractual maturity of one year or
less) and long-term (of contractual maturity of more than one year), certificates of
deposits, NDTL, total CRR requirement, etc.,
(2) Annex A to the Return in Form 'A' showing all foreign currency liabilities and
assets, and
(3) Annex B to return in Form 'A' giving details about investment in approved
securities, investment in unapproved securities, memo items such as subscription to
shares / debentures / bonds in primary market and subscriptions through private
placement.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank along with: (1) the Memorandum to the Return in Form 'A' giving details of paid-up capital, reserves, time deposits compr

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The RRB should adhere to the following practice for presentation of Reverse Repo
transactions in the Form 'A' return:
(1) Reverse Repo transactions with the banks should be reported as under:
enriched_requirements.object: practice for presentation of Reverse Repo transactions in the Form 'A' return: (1) Reverse Repo transactions with the banks sh

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Memo item 2.1 and 2.2 of Annex A to Form A (i.e., under Inter Bank Assets)
(2) The Reverse Repo transactions of a bank with non-banks (other institutions) should
be reported as under:

Whenever there are wide variations between the sources and uses of funds as
being reported in the fortnightly Return and the variations exceed 20 percent, the
banks concerned should give reasons therefor in the Return.
enriched_requirements.object: the Return

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of Regulation 5(i) (c) of the Scheduled Banks Regulations,1951, the
banks are required to furnish a list of the names, the officials designations and
specimen signatures of the officers of the banks who are authorised to sign on behalf
of the banks, Returns prescribed under Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934, and Section
18 and 24 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
enriched_requirements.object: of the names, the officials designations and specimen signatures of the officers of the banks who are authorised to sign on behalf o

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
11Under the new reporting structure, as amended vide Banking Laws
(Amendment) Act 2025, there shall be no Provisional or Final or Special Form A return.
enriched_requirements.object: Final or Special Form A return

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The banks are required to submit single Form A return.
enriched_requirements.object: A return

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The banks are required to submit
fortnightly Form A return from December 15, 2025 and monthly Form VIII return from
December 2025 on the CIMS portal.Form A and Form VIII are not to be submitted in
hard copy / paper returns by RRBs.
enriched_requirements.object: A return from December 15, 2025 and monthly Form VIII return from December 2025 on the CIMS portal
applicable, i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the banks shall
maintain minimum CRR of not less than hundred per cent of the required CRR during
the transition period.
enriched_requirements.object: not less than hundred per cent of the required CRR during the transition period

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The banks shall also submit Form A return for December 12,
2025, as per extant reporting structure.
enriched_requirements.object: return for December 12, 2025, as per extant reporting structure

conditions:
if any such day is a public holiday under the Negotiable
Instruments Act
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every RRB shall submit to the Reserve Bank before 20th day of every month, a
Return in Form VIII (Annex II) showing the amount of SLR held on last day of each
fortnight during the immediate preceding month with particulars of their DTL in India
held on such day or if any such day is a public holiday under the Negotiable
Instruments Act, 1881, at the close of business on the preceding working day.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank before 20th day of every month, a Return in Form VIII (Annex II) showing the amount of SLR held on last day of each fortnig

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Every scheduled bank shall also submit a statement as Annex to Form VIII Return
giving daily position of (a) assets held for the purpose of compliance with SLR,
enriched_requirements.object: as Annex to Form VIII Return giving daily position of (a) assets held for the purpose of compliance with SLR,

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Statutory Auditors shall verify and certify that all items of outside liabilities, as
per the bank's books had been duly compiled by the bank and correctly reflected under
12 Inserted with effect from December 15, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025 13 Inserted with effect from December 12, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025
<image>
NDTL in the fortnightly / monthly statutory returns submitted to Reserve Bank for the
financial year.
enriched_requirements.object: all items of outside liabilities, as per the bank's books had been duly compiled by the bank and correctly reflected under 12

conditions:
if the shortfall
continues on the next succeeding day/s
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(1) Penal interest shall be recovered from RRBs in the event of shortfall in
maintenance of prescribed CRR on a daily basis for that day at the rate of three
percent per annum above the Bank Rate on the amount by which the amount actually
maintained falls short of the prescribed minimum on that day and if the shortfall
continues on the next succeeding day/s, penal interest shall be recovered at the rate
of five per cent per annum above the Bank Rate.
enriched_requirements.object: in the event of shortfall in maintenance of prescribed CRR on a daily basis for that day at the rate of three percent per annum

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Banks are required to furnish the particulars such as date, amount, percentage,
reason for default in maintenance of requisite CRR and also action taken to avoid
recurrence of such default.
enriched_requirements.object: such as date, amount, percentage, reason for default in maintenance of requisite CRR and also action taken to avoid recurre

conditions:
if the default
still continues during the next succeeding fortnight
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Under the provisions of Section 42(3A) of the RBI Act, 1934, penal interest at the
increased rate of five percent above the Bank Rate become payable and if the default
still continues during the next succeeding fortnight,
(1) Every Director, Manager or Secretary of the scheduled bank who is knowingly and
willfully a party to the default, shall be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500
and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each subsequent fortnight during
which default continues.
enriched_requirements.object: which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each subsequent fortnight during which default c

conditions:
if default is made by the bank in complying with the
prohibition referred to in this clause
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The Reserve Bank may prohibit a scheduled bank from receiving any fresh deposit
after the said fortnight, and if default is made by the bank in complying with the
prohibition referred to in this clause, every director and officer of the bank who is
knowingly and willfully a party to such default or who through negligence or otherwise
contributes to such default shall in respect of each such default be punishable with
fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for
each day after the first, on which a deposit received in contravention of such prohibition
<image>
is retained by the scheduled bank.
enriched_requirements.object: such default be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each day after t

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Failure to submit the Return/late submission of the Return shall attract the
provisions of Section 42(4) of RBI Act, 1934 and banks are liable for imposition of
penalties as indicated therein.
enriched_requirements.object: Section 42(4) of RBI Act, 1934 and banks are liable for imposition of penalties as indicated therein

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
On the failure of the bank to maintain as on any day, the amount of SLR required
to be maintained by a bank, the bank shall be liable to pay to the Reserve Bank in
respect of that default, the penal interest as envisaged under Section 24 of the BR Act,
1949.
enriched_requirements.object: to the Reserve Bank in respect of that default, the penal interest as envisaged under Section 24 of the BR Act, 1949

conditions:
Where it is observed that banks are persistently defaulting despite instructions and
repeated advice
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where it is observed that banks are persistently defaulting despite instructions and
repeated advice, the Reserve Bank in addition to levy of penalty on such defaulting
banks, may be constrained to consider cancelling the licence in case of licensed
banks.
enriched_requirements.object: cancelling the licence in case of licensed banks

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The banks should, therefore, in their own interest ensure maintenance of
statutory liquidity ratio at prescribed rates and be very prompt in submission of
required Return to Regional Office concerned of Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: own interest ensure maintenance of statutory liquidity ratio at prescribed rates and be very prompt in submission of required Re

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Disclaimer: It is hereby advised that mere inclusion of any item in the above Master
Direction should not be construed as a permission to undertake all such activities
by a banking entity.
enriched_requirements.object: permission to undertake all such activities by a banking entity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions,
instructions and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these Directions shall
continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: by these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further,
the repeal of these directions, instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way
prejudicially affect:
enriched_requirements.object: affect:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be
imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in
force
enriched_requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
<image>
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of these
Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
